title: Use Filters without Carrying Essential Meaning
summary: Build and verify css filters in survey illustration and status contrast review through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css filters from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css filters without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Survey illustration and status contrast review case 1: a brightness filter reliably repairs contrast for arbitrary images and text.

Survey illustration and status contrast review case 1: use filters only for bounded visual treatment, preserve an unfiltered fallback, and remeasure contrast, legibility, and performance on actual content.
Survey illustration and status contrast review case 1: blur required instructions until hover so the form looks less crowded.

steps.title: 02 · Read the survey illustration and status contrast review case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 2: Which claim preserves css filters when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: survey illustration and status contrast review.
steps.why: Use Filters without Carrying Essential Meaning makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 2 in survey illustration and status contrast review.

content.text: Filter functions such as blur, brightness, contrast, grayscale, hue-rotate, saturate, sepia, opacity, and drop-shadow alter rendered pixels and can trigger extra compositing work. They do not understand text or image meaning and cannot reliably repair arbitrary contrast. Essential content needs an unfiltered readable path and effects need performance and preference checks. Apply it to survey illustration and status contrast review.

hints: Use Filters without Carrying Essential Meaning checkpoint 2: identify the user task and data relationship first.
